text.title: Agentic RAG Core Patterns
text.summary: Core agentic RAG architectures and the pipeline components that distinguish them from static RAG.

rule.statement: Select the architecture pattern before building: Single-Agent Router for well-defined sources, Corrective RAG (CRAG) for self-correcting accuracy, Adaptive RAG for query-complexity routing, Multi-Agent for parallel specialized retrieval.
rule.rationale: Each pattern has a distinct latency/cost/accuracy trade-off. Mixing patterns without deliberate design produces unpredictable loops.

example.note: Corrective RAG loop: retrieve → grade → rewrite (max 2x) → fallback to web search → generate → verify
Adaptive RAG: classifier routes by complexity — simple → direct, moderate → single-hop, complex → multi-hop

rule.statement: Implement all four control components: Query Router, Document Grader, Query Rewriter, Response Verifier. Skipping any one causes silent quality degradation.
rule.rationale: Without grading, irrelevant documents contaminate context. Without rewriting, failed queries loop forever or fall back immediately. Without verification, hallucinations pass undetected.

rule.statement: Use a cheap model (Haiku/gpt-4o-mini) for grading and rewriting; reserve Sonnet/Opus only for final synthesis.
rule.rationale: Grading and rewriting are classification tasks, not generation tasks. Using frontier models for them multiplies cost per query 3-5x with minimal quality gain.

example.note: Query router with structured output — routes to vectorstore, web_search, direct_response, or api

rule.statement: In multi-agent RAG, give each agent a strict input/output schema and validate at every boundary — message schema drift is the primary source of silent failures.
rule.rationale: Agents passing loosely structured text to each other accumulate interpretation errors across hops. A typed contract at each boundary catches mismatches early.

example.note: Passing unstructured agent output to the next agent without validation
example.code: # BAD: agent output passed as raw string result = agent_a.run(query) final = agent_b.run(result) # No schema validation — silent corruption

example.note: Validate agent output schema before passing downstream

text.reference: TypedDict/dataclass state schemas for CRAG, Adaptive RAG, and multi-agent workflows
Pydantic models for RouteQuery, GradeDocument, RewriteQuery, SufficiencyCheck, VerificationResult
LangGraph CRAG workflow with conditional edges
